name: Call-Of-Doodie
currentFocus: Session 56 shipped a parody-defense disclaimer footer on both home variants and kicked off a standalone-domain migration to Cloudflare Pages. Founder bought both `callofdoodie.wtf` (canonical) and `playcallofdoodie.com` (301 hedge). Migration is paused on two manual UI gates: founder must add the two zones via the Cloudflare dashboard and swap nameservers in Namecheap (API is IP-locked from this machine — current IP 45.144.114.159, allowlisted 52.124.42.65). Stored CF tokens lack zone-create permission. The next session resumes at CF Pages project creation, GitHub Actions wrangler workflow, custom-domain attachment, code base-path changes, Supabase CORS update, and old-path 301 in the apex repo.
nextMilestone: Resume domain migration in next session: create CF Pages project (`call-of-doodie`), replace `deploy.yml` with a wrangler-based GitHub Actions workflow, attach `callofdoodie.wtf` + `playcallofdoodie.com`, set Bulk Redirect from `.com` → `.wtf`, change `vite.config.js` `base` from `/call-of-doodie/` to `/`, sweep `public/sw.js` paths and bump cache `cod-v4` → `cod-v5`, update `public/manifest.json` `start_url` to `/`, add Supabase CORS allowlist for the new domain, and add a 301 redirect in `VaultSparkStudios.github.io` from the legacy `/call-of-doodie/` path. Detailed plan in `docs/DOMAIN_MIGRATION_PLAN.md`.
blockers: Cloudflare API tokens lack `com.cloudflare.api.account.zone.create` — founder must add both zones via dashboard or generate a broader-scope token
Namecheap API blocked from this machine — public IP 45.144.114.159 not in allowlist (52.124.42.65); founder must update allowlist before next migration session
